name: goal_attainment_grounding
description: Regression gate for a prose-grounding incident: capacity_planner retrieved the tenant's configured planning context (target 900, 20 hires/recruiter/month, 4 recruiters) but its PROSE substituted a generic '2-4 hires/recruiter/month' industry benchmark and asked the user for values it already had, while claims-synthesis masked the miss as a verifier 'match'. The case is scored on final_response_match_v2 (the prose-grounding signal). PRECONDITIONS for a meaningful run: (1) PROMPT_VERSION=v3.1 — only the v3.1 capacity_planner prompt carries the get_planning_context surface (the code default v3 does not), and production ships v3.1; analytics-eval.yml pins it. (2) the eval tenant (TABI_EVAL_TENANT_ID=<DEMO_TENANT_ID>, the demo tenant) already has the planning config in entitlements, so no seeding is needed. toolUses is intentionally EMPTY: compute_goal_attainment mints a random context_token, so its args can never be pinned for the blocking tool_trajectory_avg_score (threshold 1.0, IN_ORDER) — an empty expected trajectory matches trivially and lets the response-grounding judge do the real work.

parts.text: Are we on track to hit our engineering hiring target this year, and do we have enough recruiters?

Grounds the capacity analysis in the tenant's CONFIGURED planning values rather than a generic industry benchmark. States the configured annual engineering hiring target (900) and derives the team's monthly hiring capacity from the configured 4 recruiters and 20 hires/recruiter/month (about 80 hires/month). Concludes whether the current 4 recruiters are sufficient by comparing that ~80/month configured capacity against the run-rate required to reach the target. MUST NOT ask the user to supply the hiring target or the recruiter capacity/headcount (all are configured and were retrieved). MUST NOT substitute a generic '2-4 hires per recruiter per month' industry benchmark for the configured capacity, and MUST NOT conclude the team needs roughly 24 recruiters or that headcount must be increased to around 24.
